info.title: nbgv
info.description: A .NET Core Tool that can install, read and set version information based on git history, using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: install
commands.description: Prepares a project to have version stamps applied using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to the directory that should contain the version.json file. The default is the root of the git repo.

options.name: --version
options.description: The initial version to set. [default: 1.0-beta]

options.name: --source
options.description: The URI(s) of the NuGet package source(s) used to determine the latest stable version of the Nerdbank.GitVersioning package. This setting overrides all of the sources specified in the NuGet.Config files. [default: System.String[]]

commands.name: get-version
commands.description: Gets the version information for a project.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.

options.name: --metadata
options.description: Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.

options.name: --format
options.description: The format to write the version information. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.

options.name: --variable
options.description: The name of just one version property to print to stdout. When specified, the output is always in raw text. Useful in scripts.

options.name: --public-release
options.description: Specifies whether this is a public release. When specified, overrides the PublicRelease environment variable. Use --public-release=true or --public-release=false to explicitly set the value.

arguments.name: commit-ish
arguments.description: The commit/ref to get the version information for. [default: HEAD]

commands.name: set-version
commands.description: Updates the version stamp that is applied to a project.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.

commands.name: tag
commands.description: Creates a git tag to mark a version.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.

options.name: --what-if
options.description: Calculates and outputs the tag name without creating the tag. [default: False]

arguments.name: versionOrRef
arguments.description: The a.b.c[.d] version or git ref to be tagged. [default: HEAD]

commands.name: get-commits
commands.description: Gets the commit(s) that match a given version.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Use minimal output. [default: False]

commands.name: cloud
commands.description: Communicates with the ambient cloud build to set the build number and/or other cloud build variables.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory used to calculate the version. The default is the current directory. Ignored if the -v option is specified.

options.name: --metadata
options.description: Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.

options.name: --version
options.description: The string to use for the cloud build number. If not specified, the computed version will be used.

options.name: --ci-system
options.description: Force activation for a particular CI system. If not specified, auto-detection will be used. Supported values are: AppVeyor, VisualStudioTeamServices, GitHubActions, TeamCity, AtlassianBamboo, Jenkins, GitLab, Travis, SpaceAutomation, BitbucketCloud

options.name: --all-vars
options.description: Defines ALL version variables as cloud build variables, with a "NBGV_" prefix. [default: False]

options.name: --common-vars
options.description: Defines a few common version variables as cloud build variables, with a "Git" prefix (e.g. GitBuildVersion, GitBuildVersionSimple, GitAssemblyInformationalVersion). [default: False]

options.name: --skip-cloud-build-number
options.description: Do not emit the cloud build variable to set the build number. This is useful when you want to set other cloud build variables but not the build number. [default: False]

options.name: --define
options.description: Additional cloud build variables to define. Each should be in the NAME=VALUE syntax. [default: System.String[]]

commands.name: prepare-release
commands.description: Prepares a release by creating a release branch for the current version and adjusting the version on the current branch.

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.

options.name: --nextVersion
options.description: The version to set for the current branch. If omitted, the next version is determined automatically by incrementing the current version.

options.name: --versionIncrement
options.description: Overrides the 'versionIncrement' setting set in version.json for determining the next version of the current branch.

options.name: --format
options.description: The format to write information about the release. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.

options.name: --commit-message-pattern
options.description: A custom message to use for the commit that changes the version number. May include {0} for the version number. If not specified, the default is "Set version to '{0}'".

arguments.name: tag
arguments.description: The prerelease tag to apply on the release branch (if any). If not specified, any existing prerelease tag will be removed. The preceding hyphen may be omitted.
